test_description: Inject a single-character or diacritic-altering corruption into Arabic source text and verify that frozen-source comparison, downstream lineage, and study-facing surfaces all fail loudly.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: silent text corruption that survives into study units
why_this_test_matters: A one-character Arabic change can reverse meaning while remaining visually plausible.

test_description: Force alternative split/merge decisions on the same passage and check whether meaning, anchoring, omission honesty, and study usability are preserved or broken.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: unsafe boundary logic and deceptive excerpt surgery
why_this_test_matters: Excerpting is one of the highest-risk transformations in the whole pipeline.

test_description: Pick random study-facing units and require full reconstruction of source, edition/version, page, and processing path from the stored lineage alone.
why_this_test_matters: A system that cannot audit its own knowledge cannot localize future trust failures.

test_description: Construct cases where the same quoted text plays different roles in different parent sources and verify that the system classifies by current-source function, not just surface wording.
why_this_test_matters: Quoted material is a known source of scholarly misattribution.

test_description: Feed pairs of near-neighbor branches and secondary mentions to the taxonomy layer and inspect whether the system can distinguish true topic placement from mere mention.
why_this_test_matters: The tree is part of what the owner memorizes, not just an index.

test_description: Hide one proof link, one example link, and one ranking signal in otherwise plausible records, then observe whether downstream study surfaces become misleading without obvious errors.
why_this_test_matters: Locally correct content can still produce globally wrong study behavior.

test_description: Audit all study-facing views for places where confidence, uncertainty, missing lineage, or weak validation are visually suppressed or normalized.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: prestige illusion and false confidence amplification
why_this_test_matters: Polish can be the accelerator of catastrophic overtrust.

test_description: Deliberately build happy-path test suites that would pass even if the real edge rule were implemented incorrectly, then verify the harness catches the hollowness.
why_this_test_matters: A validation suite that cannot falsify bad implementations is part of the danger, not the solution.

test_description: Pick a core contract or schema and ask what breaks if one field or assumption changes; enumerate downstream consequences before build continuation.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: loose foundations and irreversible early decisions
why_this_test_matters: The project is interlinked; one weak contract can contaminate many engines.

test_description: Run corpus-scale cost and latency projections using worst-case realistic assumptions rather than demo averages.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: economic and throughput ceilings hidden by small validation sets
why_this_test_matters: A pipeline that only works on sample size is not a viable lifetime system.

test_description: Perform an end-to-end disaster recovery drill: restore the app, structured data, lineage, and searchability from backups and exports only.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: false backup confidence and non-portable recovery paths
why_this_test_matters: Backups that cannot restore working trust are not real backups.

test_description: Sample the training-candidate dataset and trace whether each record retains enough structure, lineage, and error history to be safe for future local-model use.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: training-data contamination and structurally bad saving
why_this_test_matters: Today's storage decisions become tomorrow's model behavior.

test_description: Red-team the build environment itself: missing tools, missing hooks, weak role definition, absent review routes, and unclaimed responsibility zones.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: agent-environment ceilings and coordination blind spots
why_this_test_matters: A weak builder environment silently caps system quality before any content reaches the user.

test_description: Run the same footnote-heavy source under crude policies (always glue vs always split) and compare knowledge retention, pollution, and study usability.
why_this_test_matters: Footnotes are a known zone where 'small' policy choices can reshape what knowledge survives.

test_description: Force the pipeline onto weaker fallback models for ambiguous and high-risk cases, then compare outputs and confidence signaling against the strongest path.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: silent quality-tier drift under cost or availability pressure
why_this_test_matters: Optimization pressure tends to concentrate risk in the exact cases the owner cares about most.

test_description: Introduce a defect, let it survive into multiple stored artifacts and study surfaces, then fix it and test whether the system can precisely enumerate everything contaminated during the bad period.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: inability to bound blast radius after delayed discovery
why_this_test_matters: A fix without impact localization does not restore trust.
